_notes: Auto-generated by scripts/classify_districts.py (Stage 4), then human-reviewed 2026-07-17: DIVISION 4 (Nonresidential and Mixed-Use Districts) groups the CMU/office/business/industrial base districts under one division heading, so the generator left all of it unknown. Added title-level rules splitting Sec. 24-3403 (CMU) -> mixed-use-core, Sec. 24-3404-3407 (O-1/O-2/O-3/O-S office districts) -> commercial-employment, Sec. 24-3408-3410 (B-1/B-2/B-3 business districts) -> commercial-employment, Sec. 24-3411-3413 (M-1/M-2/M-3 industrial districts) -> industrial-zone. Also added one DIVISION 5 rule for Sec. 24-3507 (UMU-PD Urban Mixed-Use Planned Development District) -> mixed-use-core -- the base CMU district (Sec. 24-3403) has almost no unconditional parcels on the ground in Henrico's live zoning layer (only 2 CMUC polygons county-wide, both address-point-adjacent to a different base district and unable to round-trip through the Census geocoder), while UMU-PD is Henrico's actually-used urban mixed-use district (e.g. the Willow Lawn corridor) and its name is unambiguous, so it was pulled out of the Division 5 catch-all. Rules are first-match. Left DIVISION 1 (general provisions), the rest of DIVISION 5 (SMX-PD/TND-PD/LI-PD -- SMX-PD 'Suburban Residential Mixed' and TND-PD 'Traditional Neighborhood Development' don't map cleanly to one vocabulary term without more judgment; LI-PD was left with its non-PD industrial siblings rather than guessed), DIVISION 6 (conditional zoning procedure), DIVISION 7 (named geographic overlays: AS-O/WBS-O/IR-O/WR-O/R5C-O/GA-O), and DIVISION 8 (FBA-O form-based overlay subdistricts) as unknown/general -- these are cross-cutting or site-specific overlay districts layered on top of a base district rather than base districts themselves, matching how other wave-2 packs (chesapeake, virginia-beach) left their equivalent overlay/PD articles unmapped. Article 4 (Use Regulations) and Article 5 (Development Standards) are genuinely cross-district standards articles (organized by use category, not by district) and are intentionally left unknown/general, same as other packs' general-standards articles.
